expectations.evidence: Axes are API-demand durability and app flatness cause; the engine's app dependency is caught and routed to verification ('a fact your own engineers can check this week... it doesn't belong on an axis').
expectations.text: choice-not-axis: the pivot decision never appears as an uncertainty axis; axes hold external unknowns like API demand converting and app trajectory

expectations.evidence: Tiles are named with short stories in Q5 and retold as a connected narrative before the table appears. The table itself lands after the recommendation, though — see the last assertion.
expectations.text: narrative-before-table: tiles arrive as told futures first; any table follows the story as recap

expectations.evidence: 'Walk in with a paid pilot already signed or in motion' — pilot signature and the two-week distribution experiment both read out before or at the meeting.
expectations.text: watchpoint-before-board: at least one watchpoint is checkable before or at the board meeting (LOI signed, paid pilot converting)

expectations.evidence: Q1 isolates the real one-way door (sunsetting, not talking to buyers); 'Amputating a working limb' premortems the wrong cut; the dual-track test carries a dated six-to-eight-week window.
expectations.text: irreversibility-respected: a premortem of the failed pivot or an explicitly phased commitment appears

expectations.evidence: The committed recommendation lands strong — then a score table and a loop note trail it, so the reply ends on 'a resourcing question for its own pass, not this one' rather than the commitment. This is the exact recap-placement gap the revised spec pins down.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-with-flips: the reply ends on a committed recommendation paired with named flip conditions
